# Contract: ICICI Reader FFI (UniFFI Swift boundary)

**Feature**: `002-icici-cc-parser` | **Date**: 2026-07-08
**Module**: `kaname-core` (Rust) â†’ `KanameCore` (Swift, generated by UniFFI)

Additive to the P1 `engine-ffi` contract. The "external interface" is the exported UniFFI
surface (not an HTTP API); generated Swift signatures are the consumer-facing form. Reuses the
P1 `Decimal`/`NaiveDate` custom types and the `Direction` enum unchanged.

---

## Exported functions

### `read_icici_statement`

| | |
|-|-|
| Rust | `#[uniffi::export] pub fn read_icici_statement(lines: Vec<String>, full_text: String) -> ParsedStatement` |
| Swift | `public func readIciciStatement(lines: [String], fullText: String) -> ParsedStatement` |

**Contract**
- Parses **already-extracted** text `lines` (one statement row per element) plus `full_text`
  (used only for enrichment). **Never** opens a file or PDF (FR-015).
- Produces one `ParsedTransaction` per matching spend line; non-matching lines are ignored
  (FR-005); rows matching the shape but failing date/amount parse go to
  `ParsedStatement.errored_lines` (FR-014) â€” **never throws, never aborts**.
- Enriches `period_end` (statement/closing date) and `card_last4` (masked PAN) from
  `full_text` when present; absent â†’ left `nil` (FR-011/012/013).
- **Pure & deterministic**: identical input â‡’ identical output; no network, clock, locale, or
  global state (FR-016/018). No failure mode surfaced to Swift (total function).

**Consumer expectations (app/test)**
- Call with PDFKit-extracted lines + full text. Treat `errored_lines` as review items, still
  consume every `lines` entry. Do not infer direction from `amount` â€” use `direction`.

### `icici_claims`

| | |
|-|-|
| Rust | `#[uniffi::export] pub fn icici_claims(full_text: String) -> bool` |
| Swift | `public func iciciClaims(fullText: String) -> Bool` |

**Contract**
- Returns `true` iff `full_text` contains the ICICI issuer marker (`"ICICI Bank"`,
  case-insensitive) â€” the document-plausibility gate (FR-001).
- MUST return `false` for other issuers' statements (e.g. an HDFC document) so a statement is
  never misattributed to ICICI (FR-002, US1-S4). Pure, deterministic, no I/O.

> Internally both wrap the pure `read_lines(&IciciReader, â€¦)` / `claims(&IciciReader, â€¦)` seam;
> the reader's `bank_code` check is fixed to `"ICICI"` for the exported `icici_claims`.

---

## Types (new records; enum reused)

### `record ParsedTransaction` (UniFFI `Record`)

```text
Rust :  struct ParsedTransaction { value_date: NaiveDate, amount: Decimal,
                                   direction: Direction, currency: String,
                                   description_raw: String, bank_code: String }
Swift:  struct ParsedTransaction { var valueDate: String;   // ISO-8601 YYYY-MM-DD
                                   var amount: Decimal;      // Foundation.Decimal (exact)
                                   var direction: Direction; // .debit / .credit
                                   var currency: String;     // "INR"
                                   var descriptionRaw: String;
                                   var bankCode: String }
```

### `record ParsedStatement` (UniFFI `Record`)

```text
Rust :  struct ParsedStatement { bank_code: String, lines: Vec<ParsedTransaction>,
                                 errored_lines: Vec<String>, period_start: Option<NaiveDate>,
                                 period_end: Option<NaiveDate>, card_last4: Option<String>,
                                 confidence: f64 }
Swift:  struct ParsedStatement { var bankCode: String; var lines: [ParsedTransaction];
                                 var erroredLines: [String]; var periodStart: String?;
                                 var periodEnd: String?; var cardLast4: String?;
                                 var confidence: Double }
```

### Reused types
- `enum Direction { Debit, Credit }` â†’ Swift `.debit` / `.credit` (P1, `uniffi::Enum`).
- Custom types `Decimal`â†”`String`â†”`Foundation.Decimal` and `NaiveDate`â†”ISO-8601 `String`
  (P1, declared in `ffi.rs` + `uniffi.toml`) â€” **unchanged**. Money never crosses as `f64`.

---

## Contract tests (test-first â€” FR-021/023)

**Rust (`cargo test`)** â€” engine-side truth (`tests/parity.rs` + unit tests):
- Golden parity: `read_icici_statement(golden.lines, golden.full_text)` equals the fixture's
  `expected` exactly (rows' date/amount/direction/currency/description_raw; `period_end`
  `2026-05-28`; `card_last4` `"1002"`; `errored_lines` empty).
- Determinism: two calls on the same input are equal.
- Wrong issuer: `icici_claims("HDFC Bank Credit Cards â€¦") == false`; `icici_claims(golden.full_text) == true`.
- Malformed row: a line matching the shape but with an unparseable date (e.g. `99/99/9999 â€¦`)
  lands in `errored_lines`, valid rows still returned, no panic.

**Swift (Swift Testing, `import KanameCore`)** â€” "core â†” Swift ICICI parse":
- Call `readIciciStatement(lines:fullText:)` over the two synthetic lines; assert 2 `lines`;
  `lines[0]` = `2026-04-29`, amount `Decimal(string:"13628.36")`, `.credit`, `"INR"`;
  `lines[1]` = `2026-05-26`, `Decimal(string:"10.20")`, `.debit`; `periodEnd == "2026-05-28"`;
  `cardLast4 == "1002"`. Assert `iciciClaims(fullText:) == true` and `false` for an HDFC string.
- Amounts compared as `Foundation.Decimal` value-equality (exact; never float).

---

## Stability / compatibility
- Adding records/functions is additive. Renaming exported symbols or changing the
  `Decimal`/`NaiveDate` wire form is breaking (requires regenerating bindings + rebuilding the
  xcframework via `make core-xcframework`, which runs **before** `tuist generate`).
- The generated `Generated/kaname_core.swift` + `KanameCoreFFI.xcframework` are build artifacts;
  **this contract**, not the generated files, is the reviewed source of truth.
